description: lUnimmMMMNMR itnmiimiinwinimiuiuiwiwmunmiM Mil GREAT FALLS DAILY TRIBUNE W M BOLE Editor OS WARDEN Manag LEONARD a DIEHL Business Manager 1 EDITORIAL PAGE SiMwwwmiiwMwiiMminBmwiimiHwwwiaMiiiimwiminuHwwwiimMninitiifliwiwiaiwBamiuwiwwiiwi iw wi iwi w i wmiiniimiiiiiiHBiiiiiiiiiiiiiHiiiiiiiwitiiiimniitmHiiiniiiitiiiiiiiwiiitiiiiiiiHiiiiimiMiiiwiiiHWiiiiowit RUSSIA AND THE ALLIES Last fall the allies withdrew their forces on the Murman coast in northwestern Russia What they ever wet there for is not clear to the or dinary mortal The excuses made at the time were rather thin and ragged But at that time things were looking pretty bad for Lenine and the bolsheviki The Red troops were defeated in the east and practically driven out of Siberia Admiral Kolchat threatened Moscow in one di rection and was almost ready to make a junc tion with the antibolshevik troops at Archangel In the south the troops of the Ukraine aided by the French were driving the Reds before them toward Moscow It looked like the end of the soviet government Now all is changed The Reds are pushing forward everywhere The Al lies have withdrawn from Archangel the French have left the Crimea The allied troops and the Slav prisoners of Austria are leaving Manchuria They ar e already pretty well out of Siberia The Red army has
